new_value: Completed: Implemented prompt input mode resolution per O1/R9. Created internal/prompt package with ResolveMode and LoadPrompt functions. Supports alias mode (config lookup), file mode (-f flag), and stdin mode (piped input). Validates all sources: missing files, unreadable files, empty content. Read-once semantics with in-memory buffering. Errors on ambiguous input (alias + file). Wired into run command. Verified: alias mode with config, file mode, stdin mode, missing file error, empty file error, unknown alias error, ambiguous input error. All acceptance criteria met.

Duplicate of ralph-rhr. The prompt source resolution and read-once buffer were implemented together in ralph-rhr. The internal/prompt package includes both ResolveMode (mode resolution) and LoadPrompt (read-once buffer with validation). No additional work needed.

old_value: {"id":"ralph-vvd","title":"Process crash recovery and loop continuity (O1/R1)","description":"Process crash handling: non-zero exit = crash; still scan buffer; same loop logic; one iteration; no retry. Task 4.3 from PLAN.md. Spec: O1/R1.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"maxdunn123@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:40:44Z","created_by":"jomadu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T04:40:44Z"}

new_value: Implemented process crash recovery. Modified SpawnAI to return exit code (0 for success, non-zero for crash). Updated IterationResult to include ExitCode field. Added crash logging in Loop (warn level for non-zero exits). Signal scanning runs on all output regardless of exit code (already implemented in ralph-3u5). Crash counts as one iteration, no retry, loop continues. Builds successfully.

Implemented consecutive failure tracking in internal/runner/loop.go. Added ExitCodeFailureThreshold error constant. Added consecutiveFailures counter to Loop function. Failure outcome increments counter and exits with ExitCodeFailureThreshold when threshold reached. No-signal outcome resets counter to 0. Success outcome exits immediately (no counter change needed). Uses cfg.Loop.FailureThreshold (default 3). Build succeeds.
{"assignee":"jomadu","status":"in_progress"}

Task 4.6 from PLAN.md. Spec: O1/R7.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"maxdunn123@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T04:40:44Z","created_by":"jomadu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T04:40:44Z"}

new_value: Implemented graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM handling per O1/R7. Added SpawnAIWithContext to spawn.go with context cancellation support: on cancel, sends SIGTERM to child, waits 5s grace period, then SIGKILL if needed. Modified Loop and RunIteration in loop.go to setup signal handlers, propagate context, detect interruption (exit 130), and discard output without signal scanning. Added ExitCodeInterrupted error and Interrupted field to IterationResult. Code compiles successfully.

new_value: Implemented distinct exit codes per O4/R1. Wired runner.Loop() execution in cmd/ralph/main.go runCmd. Added AI command resolution from alias or direct command using cmdparse.Parse(). Mapped Loop() return errors to exit codes: nil → 0 (success), ExitCodeFailureThreshold → 1, ExitCodeExhausted → 2, ExitCodeInterrupted → 130. Unexpected errors exit 1. Build succeeds. Exit code logic complete and ready for integration testing.

Implementation complete. All O2/R4 requirements satisfied: loadFromAlias checks unknown alias, missing/unreadable/empty file; loadFromFile checks missing/unreadable/empty file; loadFromStdin checks empty stdin; readFile handles file not found and permission denied. Clear error messages returned for all cases.
